basics.name: Stephen Burks
basics.label: Software Engineer II
basics.summary: Full-stack engineer with 5+ years experience shipping production features at scale. For the past 18 months I've served as acting tech lead for Columbia Sportswear's Accounts & Membership platform owning architecture, mentoring engineers, and delivering end-to-end across auth, loyalty, and observability. Before engineering I was a teacher, and the best part of both is building something that actually helps someone.

work.name: Columbia Sportswear Company
work.position: Software Engineer II, Acting Tech Lead - Accounts & Membership
work.summary: Stepped into tech lead responsibilities as the team contracted from 9 to 3; own technical direction, final reviews, and daily mentorship of offshore contractors. Leading a full rearchitecture from a legacy stack to a modern React-based platform.
highlights: Leading a full rearchitecture from a legacy stack to a modern React-based platform redesigning OAuth/SSO flows, building shared middleware, and restructuring APIs for client-side performance
Designed shared infrastructure that unblocked the migration, preserved backward compatibility, and was adopted by other teams
Built observability from scratch for authentication and account flows - caught a critical issue during what became the highest-grossing demand hour in company history
Delivered multiple international product launches end-to-end, on schedule
AI Developer Tiger Team: prototyped agent-based developer workflows and wrote the org's guidance on responsible AI tooling integration

work.name: Columbia Sportswear Company
work.summary: Core contributor on a loyalty platform migration, delivering new functionality and resolving launch-blocking issues across a complex vendor transition.
highlights: Led the end-to-end technical migration from SFRA to PWA Kit for the Accounts & Membership team, building React components and custom API endpoints with full backward-compatibility support
Architected a React-based loyalty system using custom hooks and the Context API, introducing strategic caching and state-management patterns that significantly improved data consistency
Architected the data model behind a progressive-profiling launch, enabling brand-specific personalization with minimal ongoing overhead
Co-authored the technical specification and drove performance improvements for a flagship membership relaunch that shipped on time

Contributed to a major membership re-platforming effort, migrating loyalty services and customer data to a new third-party provider while preserving a seamless experience for millions of users across 4+ brands
Invested in developer experience - tooling, documentation, and dependency health measurably improving day-to-day workflow for the broader engineering org

work.name: Hanna Andersson
work.summary: Front-end development in the SFCC ecosystem; focus on accessibility (ADA/WCAG), Core Web Vitals performance, and third-party integrations.
highlights: Built accessible, ADA-compliant user experiences using JavaScript, HTML5, and CSS3
Improved Core Web Vitals and overall site performance through targeted code optimizations
Integrated third-party services to expand business functionality and streamline user flows
Collaborated with UX/UI teams to deliver responsive, inclusive, and visually consistent web experiences

highlights: Developed WordPress websites with focus on SEO optimization and performance

work.name: Methodical Designs
work.summary: Freelance web development business specializing in WordPress solutions for small businesses.
